_readme: Consumed by workflows/triage.js's live-mcp-diagnosis search branch (only runs when args.project_id is set). Every 'sql' value is copied verbatim from the already-cited entries in references/*.md — do not hand-write new/optimized queries here. Entries with 'requiresParam' need a single placeholder token (e.g. <tableName>) substituted from the reported symptom text before running — the live-diagnosis agent is only allowed to do that one substitution, never construct new SQL. Log-service queries have no 'sql' because the MCP get_logs tool takes no filter argument — logFilterHints describe what to text-search for in the returned last-24h log lines.

cacheHitRate.sql: select 'index hit rate' as name, sum(idx_blks_hit)/nullif(sum(idx_blks_hit+idx_blks_read),0) as ratio from pg_statio_user_indexes union all select 'table hit rate' as name, sum(heap_blks_hit)/nullif(sum(heap_blks_hit)+sum(heap_blks_read),0) as ratio from pg_statio_user_tables;
cacheHitRate.interpret: ratio should be >= 0.99; a lower ratio supports 'undersized cache/compute or missing indexes' as the cause

maxConnections.interpret: establishes the hard Postgres connection ceiling; compare against pgStatActivity's live connection count to see how close the project is to it

datconnlimit.sql: select datconnlimit from pg_database where datname='postgres';
datconnlimit.interpret: anything other than -1 supports 'datconnlimit was manually misconfigured' as the cause of connection refusals

pgStatActivity.sql: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE application_name ILIKE 'pg_cron scheduler';
pgStatActivity.interpret: no row returned confirms the pg_cron scheduler process has died (needs a fast reboot); also useful more generally to see what's currently connected/running when investigating connection-limit or high-CPU symptoms

cronJobHealth.sql: SELECT * FROM cron.job_run_details WHERE status NOT IN ('succeeded','running') AND start_time > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 days' ORDER BY start_time DESC;
cronJobHealth.interpret: non-empty rows identify which specific scheduled job(s) are failing and why

roleConfig.interpret: a tight statement_timeout in rolconfig for the affected role supports 'role-level timeout is cancelling the query' as the cause

logFilterHints: postgres logs: text-search for error_severity ERROR/FATAL/PANIC combined with user_name supabase_auth_admin — confirms a 500 Auth error is DB-driven
postgres logs: text-search for 'converting NULL to string is unsupported' — confirms the direct-SQL-insert cause of 'Database error saving new user'
auth logs: text-search for SMTP/handover error messages — confirms an email-delivery misconfiguration vs. a provider/recipient-side cause
auth.notes: no queryLibrary reuse; this category is log-filter only. auth.md's 'Resolving 500 status Auth errors' entry shows a 'SQL to run in the Log Explorer' snippet (cross join unnest(metadata)...) that is BigQuery/Logflare dialect — NOT valid Postgres, do not run it via execute_sql; use get_logs(service='postgres') + text search per logFilterHints instead.

logFilterHints: postgres logs: text-search for 'infinite recursion detected in policy for relation' — confirms circular RLS policies

sqlQueries.name: httpRequestQueue
sqlQueries.interpret: non-empty result (or the query itself erroring) informs whether a clean pg_net extension reinstall is safe

rls.notes: disproportionately advisor-shaped: RLS-enabled-with-no-policy, missing index on an RLS predicate column, and the Storage INSERT/SELECT policy asymmetry are all things get_advisors can flag directly

logFilterHints: postgres logs: text-search for '42P01' or 'relation ... does not exist' to correlate with a specific table/timestamp
postgres logs: text-search for 'duplicate key violates unique constraint' to correlate with a specific sequence table

sqlQueries.name: tableExists
sqlQueries.sql: select * from information_schema.tables where table_name ilike '<tableName>';
sqlQueries.interpret: a row returned (with unexpected schema/case) supports a search-path or case-sensitivity cause; no row supports the table genuinely not existing

database-postgres.notes: richest category for queryLibrary reuse (4 of 5 shared keys apply here)

logFilterHints: postgres logs: text-search for sql_state_code 42501 to isolate the exact failing statement/user
api logs: text-search for 'PGRST002' to confirm a dropped-but-still-exposed schema

sqlQueries.name: grantsCheck
sqlQueries.sql: select grantee, privilege_type from information_schema.role_table_grants where table_name = '<tableName>';
sqlQueries.interpret: a missing SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE grant for anon/authenticated supports a grants-based 42501, as opposed to a forbidden-schema or exposed-schema-config cause

postgrest-api.notes: the postgres_logs SQL shown in postgrest-api.md's 42501 entry is BigQuery/Logflare dialect (cross join unnest(metadata)...) — NOT valid Postgres, do not run it via execute_sql; use get_logs(service='postgres') + text search instead

logFilterHints: filter returned lines by the status_code mentioned in the symptom (401/404/500/504/546)
null execution_id on a 401/404 usually means platform-level (before your code ran); a present execution_id means your code returned it
for 546: look for 'Memory limit exceeded' / 'CPU Time exceeded' in the event_message
for 504: look for consistently high execution_time_ms on the same function pathname
edge-functions.notes: richest live category overall — 5 of 10 curated entries (401/404/500/504/546) share this exact status_code + execution_id filter shape over one log service

logFilterHints: realtime logs: text-search for 'RealtimeDisabledForTenant' (project suspended for quota) or 'ChannelRateLimitReached' (TooManyChannels)
postgres logs: text-search for 'relation "objects" does not exist' (Storage search_path misconfiguration)
api logs: look for endpoints with disproportionate request volume/response size when investigating an egress symptom
realtime-storage.notes: advisorTypes targets the Storage INSERT/SELECT RLS policy asymmetry (403 on upload); heartbeat and client-side logger entries in this category are not server-observable at all

logFilterHints: text-search for 'Circuit breaker open' / 'too many authentication failures' (post-password-rotation lockout)
text-search for 'remaining connection slots reserved for non-replication superuser connections'
text-search for 'prepared_statement "..." does not exist' / 'already exists' or 'cursor/portal "..." does not exist' / 'already exists' (pooler session-artifact errors)
connections-pooling.notes: connection-limit checks recur 4x across this category's curated entries — collapsed into the shared queryLibrary keys

logFilterHints: for the 'Project Status reports unhealthy services' entry's Edge-Functions-only case: filter edge-function logs for event_message containing 'health' to check whether only health-check pings are failing (a common false positive) vs. real invocations
performance-monitoring.notes: 7 of 9 entries in this category (Grafana CPU/RAM/swap/IO chart interpretation, latency benchmarking) require dashboards the MCP tools cannot read at all — for any symptom that lands here but isn't the cache-hit-rate or unhealthy-services case, skip live checks and fall back to research-only

self-hosting-misc.notes: self-hosted infra (Kong/Docker logs) isn't project_id-scoped/MCP-reachable at all; the remaining entries are pure dashboard/CLI actions (pause/restore/region/password) with no live check to run — always research-only for this category
